situation: audit-2026-07-08-046 derived fix: calibration-math.js computeWeightsFromPairs divides by rawSum without guarding zero. When all dimErrors are Infinity (corrupted/extreme outlier data), every rawWeight = 1/(1+Inf) = 0 → rawSum = 0 → normalized[k] = 0/0 = NaN. NaN propagates through EMA blend, clamp, and final renormalization, producing all-NaN weights that silently corrupt downstream predictions.
action: Add explicit guard in computeWeightsFromPairs: when rawSum === 0 or !Number.isFinite(rawSum), fall back to uniform weights (1/N per dimension) so downstream EMA/clamp remain well-defined. Pure-function change, no DB/side-effect impact. Existing 6-test suite passes unchanged.
outcome_evidence: Commit sha 6827f69 on auto/opt-calibration-1783395468; pushed to origin. test/calibration.test.js 6/6 pass after edit. Route-fix reused existing calibration wt (no new worktree created).
next_suggestion: Next pending: SP-ENG-M-005 prediction-engine.js gaussian sigma=0 division by zero (same project, same area — may reuse opt-prediction-engine-1783395732 wt)
After M-005: SP-SCR-M-001 mail_push.py SMTP_PORT int conversion crash

audit_findings.finding: computeWeightsFromPairs normalized[k] = rawWeights[k] / rawSum produces NaN when all dimErrors are Infinity (rawSum collapses to 0). NaN propagates through EMA blend → clamp → final weight renormalization, yielding all-NaN weights that silently corrupt downstream prediction scores.
audit_findings.remediation: Guard rawSum === 0 || !Number.isFinite(rawSum): fall back to uniform weights (1/DIM_KEYS.length) so downstream stages remain well-defined.
